channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Data-driven startup Aggie uses AI to manage small business social media
item.description: A new, AI-powered platform called Aggie wants to manage your brand’s social media. Aggie is a product of Audience Genomics, which has since 2018 offered audience data analytics to big brands like Universal Studios, AMC Television, and Fenty Beauty.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Roblox debuts new child protection, parental control features
item.description: Following recent questions about child safety issues on the platform, Roblox announced Monday a number of changes designed to protect young users and increase parental control over the content they see.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Prosegur se apoya en la IA para optimizar y agilizar los procesos de selección
item.description: Desde su constitución en el año 1976, la compañía de seguridad Prosegur ha basado su crecimiento y evolución en una estrategia sustentada en tres pilares fundamentales: la innovación, su equipo humano y una orientación al cliente.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Dude Perfect went from YouTube to raising $100M. Here’s how its new CEO wants to grow the brand
item.description: Over the past 15 years, sports and entertainment company Dude Perfect—which started as a YouTube channel where five best friends posted videos of trick shots in different sports—has grown into a media juggernaut with attendant cruises, toy lines, and TV deals.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Gagne, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s new DOGE task force is tailor-made to benefit Elon Musk’s businesses
item.description: On Tuesday, President-elect Donald Trump named Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy as cochairs of a new Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: What you need to know about brain-training games
item.description: Some 2.3 million U.S. adults over 65—more than 4%—have a diagnosis of dementia. But even without a diagnosis, a certain amount of cognitive decline is normal as age sets in.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s AirPods Pro 2 makes hearing tests as easy as a few taps
item.description: Checking your hearing has gotten easier with the new hearing test feature in AirPods Pro 2. You can take reliable hearing tests using your iPhone or iPad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built
item.description: A new challenge looks to digitally recreate the planning of each module of the International Space Station. The post A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: AI, cybersecurity drive IT investments and lead skill shortages for 2025
item.description: AI and cybersecurity are driving enterprise tech investments and at the same time exposing the talent gaps that IT teams face.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Salesforce: Latest news and insights
item.description: Salesforce is a vendor of cloud-based software and applications for sales, customer service, marketing automation, ecommerce, analytics, and application development. Based in San Francisco, Calif.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Strategies to navigate the pitfalls of cloud costs
item.description: In my line of work, I often hear CIOs and CFOs say, “Cloud providers are bleeding us dry.” As ever-increasing cloud bills roll in, they’re demanding answers about what they’re paying for and why they’re paying so much. Public cloud providers are eager to oppose this line of questioning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Solidigm unveils 122TB data center SSD for AI workloads
item.description: Solidigm, a provider of NAND flash solid-state drives, and memory giant Micron have each introduced very high-capacity SSD drives meant for enterprise data centers, particularly in AI use cases. Traditional hard disk drives have maintained their viability because of capacity advantages over SSDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Now you can download an ISO file of Windows 11 for Arm chips
item.description: It’s been possible for users to download ISO files of the Windows operating system, but until now that option has only applied to the x86 version. Now it’s finally possible to download an ISO file of Windows 11 for computers with Arm-based chips from Microsoft’s website, according to Neowin.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EU slaps Meta with a nearly 800 million euro fine for engaging in 'abusive' Marketplace practices
item.description: European Union regulators hit Facebook parent Meta with a fine of nearly 800 million euros on Thursday for what it calls “abusive practices” involving its Marketplace online classified ads business.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Solar-powered aircraft achieves groundbreaking 22-hour autonomous flight
item.description: Skydweller is the world's largest unmanned solar-powered aircraft that can stay airborne for weeks, and even months, without refueling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Observe, New Relic boost observability of Kubernetes workloads
item.description: Observability providers Observe and New Relic this week separately introduced Kubernetes-specific capabilities in their platforms that will enable faster problem resolution across complex container environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Your email didn’t expire; it’s just another sneaky scam
item.description: Scammers are using increasingly sophisticated ways to lure unsuspecting victims, and some are impersonating an email help desk or support team.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11
